items.title: CPI inflation soars by most since 2022 as gas prices biteHeadline inflation clocked in at 3.3% on a year ago, a rapid acceleration from February's levels.
Stocks edge up after inflation heats up, with Iran talks in focus
Has Hormuz reopened after Trump's demands? Where things stand.
Consumer sentiment sinks to record low as war tanks economic outlook
Why Shaquille O'Neal is investing his money in an adult toy store
TSMC sales smash record as war fails to dent AI demand
Why the Fed is likely to hold on rates despite soaring energy prices
March CPI breakdown: Iran war sends gas prices skyrocketing
Anthropic and CoreWeave sign AI agreement, as chip deals explode
Snap unit to use Qualcomm chips for upcoming AI smart glasses
Space analytics firm HawkEye's revenue jumped 74% in 2025, US IPO filing shows
Analysis-Iran war leaves crisis-scarred countries counting the cost
Goldman’s ‘Unwavering’ Support Shows Gulf’s Lure for Wall Street
Meta must face youth addiction lawsuit by Massachusetts, court rules
Iran war oil shock pushes US inflation to highest level in almost two years
Demand for Dollars in Swaps Ebbs Alongside Global Risks
Americans close to retirement are saving for later, but they're worried about getting by right now

We're living in a 'whiplash economy': Claudia Sahm on CPI dataNew Century Advisors chief economist Claudia Sahm and UBS Global Wealth Management head of taxable fixed income strategy Leslie Falconio chat with Yahoo Finance's Julie Hyman and Myles Udland about the breaking data. Sahm also served as a Federal Reserve Board economist and is the creator of the central bank's Sahm Rule recession indicator.
Bitcoin climbs above $72K following March's hot inflation data
Inflation rose 0.9% in March amid energy price shocks

Consumer sentiment sinks to record low in April as US-Iran tanks economic outlookConsumer sentiment in April fell sharply to a record low as high energy prices and market volatility weighed on American households.
Inflation surged in March as Iran war drove up energy costsInflation rose at an annual rate of 3.3% in March, driven by the sharpest monthly increase in gas prices since 1967.

US inflation surges to 3.3% as Iran war fuels energy price shockUS consumer prices rose sharply in March, with headline inflation climbing to 3.3% year-on-year as energy costs spiked in the wake of the Iran war, official data showed on Friday.View on euronews

CPI inflation soars by most since 2022 as gas prices riseHeadline CPI inflation clocked in 3.3% higher than a year ago while being up 0.9% on a monthly basis, a rapid acceleration from February's levels.

Gap between high-income households' wage growth and everyone else is at its widest since 2015After-tax wage growth is accelerating faster among higher-income households as other households trail behind, a new Bank of America Institute report shows.

IMF head says global growth outlook is lower due to war in IranInternational Monetary Fund managing director Kristalina Georgieva said Thursday that the fund has lowered its global growth outlook even in the most optimistic scenario on account of the conflict in the Middle East.

Fed's March minutes: Officials saw Iran war increasing inflation, delaying rate cutsFederal Reserve officials expected that higher oil prices resulting from the Iran war would increase inflation in the near term and delay price inflation falling back toward their 2% goal, according to minutes from the central bank’s March policy meeting released Wednesday.

Dallas Fed study: Inflation expectations could improve quickly if Strait of Hormuz reopensWith a two-week ceasefire underway in Iran, oil prices are dropping. If the situation holds, expectations for higher inflation could also drop.

Fed's vice chair says oil price shock complicates inflation outlookFederal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson said Tuesday that the jolt in energy prices complicates his inflation outlook, and depending on how long the Middle East conflict lasts, elevated oil prices could weigh on consumer and business spending.

US households see their finances deteriorating amid rising gas pricesMore US households anticipate they'll be financially worse off in the year ahead as gas prices increase amid the US-Israel war on Iran, according to the New York Federal Reserve's March survey of consumer expectations.
